source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In figure, \( m_1 = 20.0 \text{ kg} \) and \( \alpha = 53.1^\circ \). The coefficient of kinetic friction between the block of mass \( m_1 \) and the incline is \( \mu_{\text{k}} = 0.40 \). What must be the mass \( m_2 \) of the hanging block if it is to descend \( 12.0 \text{ m} \) in the first \( 3.00 \text{ s} \) after the system is released from rest?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly begins with 'In figure', indicating dependence on a diagram
the physical setup involves an incline and hanging block arrangement, which is typically diagram-dependent
multi_step: question requires using displacement=12.0 m, time=3.00 s, and released from rest to infer motion/acceleration
question then combines that result with force relations involving m1, incline angle alpha, and kinetic friction mu_k
unknown target is m2, so solving requires chaining at least kinematics plus dynamics
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric target ('36.0 kg')
multiple-choice options are provided, which makes final-answer checking straightforward
given quantities are explicit in the text (m1, alpha, mu_k, distance, time), so a rule-based or symbolic check is feasible
